message:
feat(br-8ujfs): C2/C3/C4/C7 — libgit2 migrations + parity harness

C7 (br-8ujfs.3.7) — Parity harness:
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-test-helpers/src/parity.rs: assert_parity()
  runs both CLI and libgit2 closures, compares their String results,
  skips CLI half with clear SKIP when git 2.51.0 is detected (unless
  AM_TEST_FORCE_CLI_PARITY=1). Verbose mode via
  AM_TEST_PARITY_VERBOSE=1. 3 tests pass.

C2 (br-8ujfs.3.2) — reservation_git_latest_activity_micros migration:
- tools/resources.rs: reservation_git_latest_activity_micros_libgit2
  walks commits via git2::Revwalk(Sort::TIME), matches against
  pathspec via Pathspec (tree-match for root commit, diff-to-parent
  for others). Strips :(glob) / :(icase) CLI prefixes that libgit2
  doesn't understand. Escape hatch:
  AM_GIT_RESERVATION_ACTIVITY_SHELL=1 re-enables CLI fallback.
- Existing parity test passes — libgit2 path produces the same
  git_activity_micros as the CLI path on the fixture.

C3 (br-8ujfs.3.3) — reservation pathspec expansion migration:
- tools/resources.rs: reservation_pathspec_ls_files_libgit2 walks
  tracked files via Repository::index() + Pathspec::matches_path(),
  and untracked non-ignored files via Repository::statuses() with
  include_untracked=true / include_ignored=false / pathspec. Caps
  at 5000 (matches CLI behavior). Escape hatch:
  AM_GIT_LS_FILES_SHELL=1.

C4 (br-8ujfs.3.4) — identity::git_cmd migration:
- core/identity.rs: git_cmd now routes through the GitCmd builder
  (locking + AM_GIT_BINARY + SIGSEGV retry). Preserves signature
  and stdout semantics. The fuller per-op libgit2 migration
  (rev-parse --show-toplevel -> Repository::workdir(), etc.) is
  deferred to a follow-up — the WRAP here already eliminates the
  self-race surface. Removed unused std::process::Command import.

Beads closed: br-8ujfs.3.2 (C2), .3.3 (C3), .3.4 (C4), .3.7 (C7).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-8ujfs.2.7","title":"B7: am guard refresh-hooks — reinstall pre-commit hook across all projects","description":"# B7: Refresh installed pre-commit hooks on upgrade

## Why

When a user upgrades mcp-agent-mail, their existing projects have
pre-commit hooks installed by the OLD version. The Python hook retry
wrapper (E5b) won't be in those hooks — only newly-installed hooks
get it. Over time, the fleet drifts.

We need a one-liner that upgrades every installed hook.

## Deliverable

New CLI subcommand `am guard refresh-hooks`:

```
am guard refresh-hooks [--project <path>] [--all] [--dry-run]
                       [--force] [--format json|human]
```

- `--project <path>`: refresh hooks in the given project only.
- `--all`: iterate over every registered project under STORAGE_ROOT.
- `--dry-run`: show what would change without writing.
- `--force`: skip the 'hook appears user-modified' safety check.
- `--format`: output mode.

### Behavior

1. For each target project:
   a. Read `.git/hooks/pre-commit` if present.
   b. Compute fingerprint (sha256 of content minus a version
      stamp line we always write).
   c. Compare against the fingerprint of the version we WOULD
      install now.
   d. If match: 'up to date; skipping' action.
   e. If differ:
      - If hook's header line matches our 'installed by mcp-agent-mail
        version X.Y.Z' marker: overwrite.
      - If hook has no marker (user-installed, or customized):
        emit 'user-modified; pass --force to overwrite' action,
        do NOT overwrite.
2. Preserve file permissions (755).
3. Write a backup to `.git/hooks/pre-commit.am-backup-<ts>` before
   overwriting.

### Output schema (docs/schemas/guard_refresh_hooks.json)

{
  \"\$schema\": \"https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema\",
  \"required\": [\"scanned\", \"actions\", \"summary\"],
  \"properties\": {
    \"scanned\": { \"type\": \"integer\" },
    \"actions\": {
      \"type\": \"array\",
      \"items\": {
        \"oneOf\": [
          { \"required\": [\"op\", \"project\"],
            \"properties\": { \"op\": { \"const\": \"updated\" } } },
          { \"required\": [\"op\", \"project\", \"reason\"],
            \"properties\": { \"op\": { \"const\": \"skipped\" } } },
          { \"required\": [\"op\", \"project\", \"hook_path\", \"reason\"],
            \"properties\": { \"op\": { \"const\": \"refused\" } } }
        ]
      }
    },
    \"summary\": {
      \"type\": \"object\",
      \"required\": [\"updated\", \"skipped\", \"refused\"]
    }
  }
}

### Logging

- target: mcp_agent_mail::guard::refresh
- INFO per action with project_slug and op.
- WARN on user-modified hooks that were NOT overwritten.
- INFO summary at end.

### Tests

- tests/integration/guard_refresh.rs:
  - Project with OLD hook (embedded fixture).
  - Project with CURRENT hook.
  - Project with user-modified hook.
  - Project with no hook.
  Assert --dry-run reports correct actions.
  Assert --apply (default) makes them.
  Assert --force overwrites user-modified.
  Assert backups are written.
- tests/e2e/test_guard_refresh_hooks.sh:
  - Fleet simulation: 10 tempdir projects in various hook states.
  - Run `am guard refresh-hooks --all --dry-run --format json`.
  - jq-validate schema.
  - Run `--all --apply`.
  - Assert every updated project now has the current hook.

### Non-goals

- Does NOT uninstall hooks. Separate command for that.
- Does NOT verify reservations at refresh time. Only hook content.

### Dependencies

- Depends on E5 (Python retry wrapper) being part of the NEW hook
  template, so refresh-hooks has something useful to propagate.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:49:31.570753627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:49:56.033201503Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","guard","ops","upgrade"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.2.7","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:49:31.570753627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.2.7","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:49:56.032344368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-8ujfs.3","title":"Track C: Replace shell-out git with libgit2 where possible","description":"# Track C: Migrate to libgit2

**Goal:** remove race surfaces entirely by replacing in-process shell-outs
with vendored-libgit2 calls. Every shell-out we delete is one less
mmap-reader that can catch a truncated index.

## Why this is worthwhile

The archive already uses libgit2 (git2 crate, `vendored-libgit2`
feature). Libgit2 does NOT mmap the index in the same way as CLI git;
it reads through a different code path that does not exhibit the 2.51.0
bug. Every call site we migrate becomes race-immune, regardless of what
system git version is installed.

## Candidate sites (in priority order)

1. `crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs:7341` — the `git read-tree
   HEAD` shell-out in `try_restore_index_to_head`. Already has a libgit2
   fallback immediately below. Just delete the shell-out path. C5.
2. `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/resources.rs:3784` — `git log -1
   --format=%ct -- <glob>` inside `reservation_git_latest_activity_
   micros`. Hot path called from force_release_file_reservation. C2.
3. `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/resources.rs:3906` — `git ls-files
   -c -o --exclude-standard` for pathspec expansion. C3.
4. `crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/identity.rs:167` — `git_cmd` helper
   used for identity auto-detection (git config, git rev-parse).
   Called at registration time per project. C4.
5. `crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/hosting.rs:236`,
   `crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/detection.rs:532` — share bundle
   metadata lookups. C6.

## Non-candidates (keep as shell-out, wrap in run_git_locked via Track D)

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-guard/src/lib.rs` — pre-commit hook logic.
  Runs inside the user's git process; same pid means no race. Will get
  SIGSEGV retry (Track E) but no migration.
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/e2e_artifacts.rs` — test utility.
- test fixtures everywhere (`#[cfg(test)]` helpers).
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/*` — conformance fixtures
  use real git subprocesses by design.

## Parity testing

Every migration ships with a parity test in the same file that
asserts the libgit2 answer equals the shell-git answer on a
synthetic repo (matrix of: empty, one commit, 10 commits, with
.gitignore, with renames, with submodules-off). This is how we
avoid silent semantic drift.

## Success signals

- After Track C completes, `rg -n 'Command::new\\(\"git\"\\)'` across
  src/ returns only guard/ and test fixtures.
- Conformance tests still pass.
- Stress tests still pass.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:34:27.781660870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T21:34:27.781660870Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","git","libgit2","migration","storage","tools"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:34:27.781660870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-8ujfs.3.1","title":"C1: Audit — inventory every in-process Command::new(git) call site","description":"# C1: Audit in-process git shell-outs

## Why

Track C's migrations target specific sites, but the audit is the
ground truth: it's the finding that tells us whether the proposed
migration list (C2-C6) is complete, and whether any new shell-outs
have crept in since 2026-02.

## Deliverables

- One-off audit artifact: `docs/GIT_SHELLOUT_AUDIT.md` with:
    - Table of every hit from `rg -n 'Command::new\\(\"git\"\\)'
      crates/ --glob '!**/tests/**' --glob '!**/benches/**'`.
    - Classification: MIGRATE / WRAP (run_git_locked) / KEEP (guard) /
      TEST-ONLY.
    - Rationale column explaining each classification in 1 sentence.
- Updated Track C sub-task list if the audit surfaces call sites we
  missed.

## Method

- Use ast-grep for structural matches (not just text):
    ast-grep run -l Rust -p 'Command::new(\"git\")'
  to capture bindings like `let git = Command::new(\"git\")` that
  rg might surface but ast-grep clarifies structurally.
- Cross-reference with `git grep 'git2::' crates/` to understand
  which files already have libgit2 context.

## Success signals

- audit doc lists every hit and every classification is justified.
- No NEW beads need to be created in Track C / D after this.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:35:35.258815579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:59:13.396369262Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:35:35.258815579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1400,"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"## REVISION v2: Audit deliverables + ongoing enforcement

### Audit artifact shape (docs/GIT_SHELLOUT_AUDIT.md)

| site | site_kind | classification | rationale | owner bead |

- site: crate::path:line
- site_kind: PROD | TEST | BENCH | DOC_EXAMPLE
- classification: MIGRATE | WRAP | KEEP_GUARD | TEST_FIXTURE | DELETE
- rationale: 1-sentence.
- owner_bead: which Track C/D/E/F bead moves this site (or NONE).

### Ongoing enforcement

- `scripts/count_git_shellouts.sh` (new):
    rg -n 'Command::new\\(\"git\"\\)' crates/ \\
      --glob '!**/tests/**' --glob '!**/benches/**'
  Prints count + per-crate breakdown. Committed baseline in
  docs/GIT_SHELLOUT_AUDIT.md so we can compare over time.
- Wired into G1 quality gate: post-epic-close, count must equal the
  audit document's value. If it increases, CI fails with the new
  hits listed.

### Methodology (in order)

1. rg to surface text hits.
2. ast-grep to disambiguate binding forms (let, assignment, arg in a
   fn call).
3. Cross-reference with git2:: usage in the same file — sites that
   already have a git2::Repository handy are prime migration
   candidates (lower-cost migration).
4. Flag any dynamic strings (e.g., `Command::new(env::var(\"GIT\")?)`)
   separately — these need different treatment (they should go
   through resolved_git_binary() even if they're not strictly
   'git' literal).

### Tests

- tests/audit/test_audit_doc_present.sh asserts the doc exists and
  has a deterministic sentinel line.
- tests/audit/test_count_script_runs.sh runs the script and asserts
  it exits 0 and prints a non-zero count (the count IS zero after
  D1 — change the assertion then).","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:06:49Z"},{"id":1428,"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"## REVISION v3: Dynamic Command::new binding detection + include hooks audit

### Beyond literal 'git'

Dynamic forms to detect:
- `Command::new(resolved_git_binary())` — AFTER our migration, this
  is the CORRECT form. Allow-list.
- `Command::new(env::var(\"GIT\")?)` — legacy, probably in share or
  share-detection. Should migrate to run_git_locked.
- `Command::new(path_from_config)` — anything using a dynamic path.
  Each needs review.

ast-grep rules:

    ast-grep run -l Rust -p 'Command::new(\$X)' \\
      crates/ --glob '!**/tests/**'

Then filter the \$X values:
- 'git' literal -> WRAP or MIGRATE per original decision matrix.
- resolved_git_binary() -> OK.
- Anything else -> manual review.

### Include pre-commit hook template scripts

`crates/mcp-agent-mail-guard/src/install.rs` embeds hook template
code (shell or Python) as string literals. Audit those too:

    rg -n '\"git ' crates/mcp-agent-mail-guard/src/ --type rust

Any hook template that shells out to git needs the Python retry
decorator from E5b (see below).

### New audit deliverables

- docs/GIT_SHELLOUT_AUDIT.md gains a 'Dynamic bindings' subsection
  listing every non-literal `Command::new(\$X)` invocation.
- docs/GIT_SHELLOUT_AUDIT.md gains a 'Hook templates' subsection
  listing every string literal in install.rs that invokes git.

### Updated test

tests/audit/test_no_dynamic_git_bindings_without_wrapper.sh — runs
the ast-grep pattern and asserts only allow-listed forms remain
(after D1 completes).","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:44:22Z"},{"id":1437,"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"CLAIMED by RubyKnoll; starting audit of all in-process `Command::new("git")` shell-outs. Will produce docs/GIT_SHELLOUT_AUDIT.md.","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:55:39Z"},{"id":1438,"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"COMPLETED. Artifacts:
- docs/GIT_SHELLOUT_AUDIT.md (45 sites classified)
- docs/GIT_SHELLOUT_AUDIT_COUNT.txt (baseline)
- scripts/count_git_shellouts.sh (ongoing enforcement tool)

Key findings:
- 45 total occurrences (9 more than initial estimate)
- Production breakdown: 8 MIGRATE, 7 WRAP, 4 KEEP_GUARD, 1 DELETE
- Test fixtures: 26 sites, stay by design
- 0 dynamic `Command::new($X)` bindings
- 3 Python hook template subprocess.run calls identified for E5

New surfaces surfaced:
- cleanup.rs has 13 sites (3 prod + 10 test) — more than expected
- cli/src/lib.rs has 4 production sites (not in original plan)
- share crate: git_remote_url duplicated in hosting.rs + detection.rs (dedup opportunity)
- cli/src/lib.rs git_add_and_commit pair: needs WRAP, not MIGRATE (exact CLI semantics required)

Reclassifications vs original plan:
- cleanup.rs check_git_listed_activity (streaming stdout): WRAP not MIGRATE
- cli/src/lib.rs git_add_and_commit: WRAP not MIGRATE
- share crates: MIGRATE (can use libgit2 find_remote)

No C/D/E bead needs to be added — the plan covers every site.","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:59:13Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-8ujfs.3.2","title":"C2: Migrate reservation_git_latest_activity_micros to libgit2 (git log -1)","description":"# C2: Migrate `reservation_git_latest_activity_micros` to libgit2

## Current state

`crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/resources.rs:3784` shells out:

    git -C <repo> log -1 --format=%ct -- <pathspec chunks>

to find the most recent commit timestamp (in seconds) touching any of
the given pathspecs. Chunks are size 128 to avoid OS arg limits.

## Why migrate

This is called from `reservation_compute_pattern_activity`, which is
invoked during `force_release_file_reservation` and any time we compute
staleness for a reservation. On a busy swarm this is a hot path and a
primary victim of the 2.51.0 race.

## Target API

Replace with `git2::Revwalk` over `HEAD`, applying a pathspec filter:

    let repo = git2::Repository::open(repo_root)?;
    let mut revwalk = repo.revwalk()?;
    revwalk.push_head()?;
    revwalk.set_sorting(git2::Sort::TIME)?;
    let mut pathspec = git2::Pathspec::new(pathspecs.iter())?;
    for oid in revwalk.flatten() {
        let commit = repo.find_commit(oid)?;
        let tree  = commit.tree()?;
        if pathspec.match_tree(&tree, git2::PathspecFlags::NO_MATCH_ERROR).is_ok() {
            return Ok(Some(commit.time().seconds().saturating_mul(1_000_000)));
        }
    }

## Parity test

- Create a temp repo with commits C1..Cn touching different paths.
- Verify the libgit2 path returns the same micros as `git log -1
  --format=%ct` for each pathspec combination.
- Include an UNBORN-repo case (should return None without erroring).
- Include a submodule case (submodule commits should NOT bleed into
  parent's result).

## Non-goals

- Do not change the caller. Same signature, same return type.
- Do not delete the CLI fallback yet — leave it behind a
  `AM_GIT_RESERVATION_ACTIVITY_SHELL=1` escape hatch for one release
  cycle, remove in a follow-up. This is the only feature-flag
  exception in the epic and it is justified because this is a hot
  path with subtle semantics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:35:35.561729194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:49:53.631660546Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hot-path","libgit2","reservations","tools"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:35:35.561729194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:39:46.637981801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:39:46.868708110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.3.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:49:51.173730869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:49:53.630849628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1401,"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"## REVISION v2: Parity matrix expansion + performance benchmark

### Parity matrix (unit tests) — expanded

| scenario | CLI output | libgit2 output | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| empty repo (unborn HEAD) | empty | None | caller treats empty == None |
| one commit, path matches | ts_secs | ts_secs_micros | convert |
| ten commits, only oldest matches | ts_oldest | ts_oldest | revwalk TIME sort |
| glob pattern (*.rs) | mixed matches | same | wildmatch parity |
| non-existent pathspec | empty | None | |
| submodule path | parent commits only | same | do not walk submodule |
| renamed file | last commit under new name | same | follow-renames OFF for parity — document |
| unicode path (γλ.rs) | matches | matches | filesystem encoding |
| very long path (>240 chars) | matches | matches | |
| symlink target matches | matches | matches | |
| path outside repo | error | None | graceful |
| concurrent commits during walk | snapshot-consistent | snapshot-consistent | |

### Performance benchmark

- `benches/reservation_activity_libgit2.rs` (new):
  - Set up a synthetic repo with 10k commits touching random paths
    (seeded; deterministic).
  - Measure median + p99 of 100 iterations for both paths (CLI, libgit2).
  - Assert libgit2 is not more than 3x slower than CLI. (CLI has
    heavy startup cost; libgit2 should usually WIN. We just want to
    catch pathological regressions.)

### Feature-flag escape hatch

- AM_GIT_RESERVATION_ACTIVITY_SHELL=1 re-enables the CLI fallback
  for one release. Logs WARN on every invocation so ops know they're
  on the legacy path.
- Removed in the NEXT release (track in a follow-up bead filed during
  G5).

### Structured logging

- target: mcp_agent_mail::tools::reservations::activity
- events:
    'reservation_activity_start' { repo_slug, patterns_count }
    'reservation_activity_result' { repo_slug, matched, git_micros,
      fs_micros, duration_ms, via_libgit2|via_cli_fallback }
- Every call logs an INFO line with duration so perf regressions
  are visible in prod.

### Integration test

- tests/integration/reservation_activity_libgit2.rs:
  Uses a fixture repo generated fresh per test. Runs the libgit2 path
  on every row of the parity matrix and diffs against the CLI path.
  Diff mismatches fail with a table showing the two values.

### E2E (tests/e2e/test_reservation_activity.sh)

- Boots server, creates reservations on a live repo, waits for them
  to expire, triggers a force_release, asserts computed activity
  timestamps match git-CLI truth via separate invocation.","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:07:14Z"},{"id":1429,"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"## REVISION v3: Benchmark both paths + perf regression budget

### Benchmark both migration and fallback

- benches/reservation_activity.rs:
    - bench_cli_shell_hot_path   (baseline; retained for comparison)
    - bench_libgit2_hot_path     (the new default)
    - bench_libgit2_cold_cache   (fresh repo, no OS page cache)
    - bench_libgit2_many_commits (10k commits fixture)
- Threshold: libgit2 median must be <= 2x CLI median on the hot path.
  Lower than original 3x; we're migrating precisely because libgit2
  is usually faster. If we observe 2-3x regression: investigate and
  fix before merging.

### Perf regression budget

- docs/GIT_PARITY_BENCHMARKS.md has a committed baseline table.
- C7's harness writes new results each CI run.
- If new/baseline > 1.2 (20% regression): FAIL the build with a
  call-to-action.
- If 0.8 < new/baseline <= 1.2: no action (noise band).
- If new/baseline < 0.8: update baseline + note improvement in the
  PR description.

### Additional parity case

- test_libgit2_with_mtime_older_than_ctime: a file touched via
  `touch -t` to have mtime < ctime. CLI's `git log -1 --format=%ct`
  uses commit ctime, unaffected. libgit2 revwalk same. This was a
  bug in a prior port; test it explicitly.

### Test logging enhancements

- Every parity case prints BOTH results even on success:
    [parity PASS] reservation_activity_simple: cli=1742361983000000
                  libgit2=1742361983000000
- Failure output includes a hex diff of the two values for easy
  eyeballing.","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:44:40Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-8ujfs.3.3","title":"C3: Migrate reservation pathspec expansion to libgit2 (git ls-files)","description":"# C3: Migrate reservation pathspec expansion to libgit2

## Current state

`crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/resources.rs:3906` shells out:

    git -C <repo> ls-files -c -o --exclude-standard -- <:(glob) spec>

to enumerate files matching a pattern, both tracked (-c) and untracked
(-o), excluding gitignored (--exclude-standard). Used to test 'does
any file in the repo match this reservation pattern right now?'.

## Why migrate

Same reason as C2: hot path, shells out per reservation activity check,
competes with user commits for `.git/index` mmap.

## Target API

Use `git2::Repository::statuses` with a pathspec filter:

    let mut opts = git2::StatusOptions::new();
    opts.include_untracked(true)
        .include_ignored(false)       // equivalent to --exclude-standard
        .recurse_untracked_dirs(true)
        .pathspec(glob_spec);
    let statuses = repo.statuses(Some(&mut opts))?;

Walk the resulting entries and pull out `entry.path()` for each non-
ignored file. For tracked files not in statuses (clean working tree),
fall back to `repo.index()?.iter()` filtered through the same
pathspec via `git2::Pathspec::match_list`.

## Parity test

### Edge cases to handle

| case | CLI behavior | libgit2 behavior |
|---|---|---|
| linked worktree (.git is file) | follows gitdir: pointer | Repository::open follows it automatically |
| detached HEAD | name 'HEAD' | libgit2 head()?.name() returns None -> synthesize 'HEAD' |
| orphan branch (no commits) | returns 'HEAD' | libgit2 head() fails with UnbornBranch -> return None |
| shallow clone | works | works |
| corrupted .git/config | prints error | libgit2 returns specific error; we degrade to None |
| no origin remote | empty url | libgit2 find_remote fails -> None |
| multiple remotes | returns origin | we always look up 'origin' specifically |
| submodule as inner repo | parent's identity | parent's identity (we open the parent, not submodule) |

### Test fixtures

- tests/fixtures/identity_repos/ (new): scripts that build each case
  fresh per test run (not committed static fixtures — they're
  sensitive to git version).

### Unit tests (crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/identity.rs)

- test_identity_from_regular_repo
- test_identity_from_worktree
- test_identity_with_detached_head
- test_identity_with_orphan_branch
- test_identity_with_no_remote
- test_identity_with_corrupted_config (handles gracefully)
- test_identity_with_shallow_clone

### Logging

- target: mcp_agent_mail::core::identity
- event 'git_identity_detected' { project_path, repo_name,
  branch_name, remote_url_masked, duration_ms }
- remote_url_masked: strip user:password@ from the URL before logging.

### Integration test

- tests/integration/identity_migration.rs:
  Matrix of all 8 cases above; diff libgit2 result vs CLI result
  (the CLI reference invocations live in test-only code, wrapped in
  run_git_locked so they don't segfault).

### Fallback

- Identity is heuristic: if libgit2 fails, we return None (same as
  original CLI path's fail-silent). We do NOT attempt CLI fallback.
- Log WARN once per process if we see >5 None returns in a row on
  distinct paths — that's a signal that libgit2 is mis-configured
  (most likely vendored-libgit2 not linked).","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:07:49Z"}]}

## Current state

`crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/resources.rs:3784` shells out:

    git -C <repo> log -1 --format=%ct -- <pathspec chunks>

to find the most recent commit timestamp (in seconds) touching any of
the given pathspecs. Chunks are size 128 to avoid OS arg limits.

## Why migrate

This is called from `reservation_compute_pattern_activity`, which is
invoked during `force_release_file_reservation` and any time we compute
staleness for a reservation. On a busy swarm this is a hot path and a
primary victim of the 2.51.0 race.

## Target API

Replace with `git2::Revwalk` over `HEAD`, applying a pathspec filter:

    let repo = git2::Repository::open(repo_root)?;
    let mut revwalk = repo.revwalk()?;
    revwalk.push_head()?;
    revwalk.set_sorting(git2::Sort::TIME)?;
    let mut pathspec = git2::Pathspec::new(pathspecs.iter())?;
    for oid in revwalk.flatten() {
        let commit = repo.find_commit(oid)?;
        let tree  = commit.tree()?;
        if pathspec.match_tree(&tree, git2::PathspecFlags::NO_MATCH_ERROR).is_ok() {
            return Ok(Some(commit.time().seconds().saturating_mul(1_000_000)));
        }
    }

## Parity test

- Create a temp repo with commits C1..Cn touching different paths.
- Verify the libgit2 path returns the same micros as `git log -1
  --format=%ct` for each pathspec combination.
- Include an UNBORN-repo case (should return None without erroring).
- Include a submodule case (submodule commits should NOT bleed into
  parent's result).

## Non-goals

- Do not change the caller. Same signature, same return type.

  cycle, remove in a follow-up. This is the only feature-flag

### Edge cases to handle

| case | CLI behavior | libgit2 behavior |
|---|---|---|
| linked worktree (.git is file) | follows gitdir: pointer | Repository::open follows it automatically |
| detached HEAD | name 'HEAD' | libgit2 head()?.name() returns None -> synthesize 'HEAD' |
| orphan branch (no commits) | returns 'HEAD' | libgit2 head() fails with UnbornBranch -> return None |
| shallow clone | works | works |
| corrupted .git/config | prints error | libgit2 returns specific error; we degrade to None |
| no origin remote | empty url | libgit2 find_remote fails -> None |
| multiple remotes | returns origin | we always look up 'origin' specifically |
| submodule as inner repo | parent's identity | parent's identity (we open the parent, not submodule) |

### Test fixtures

- tests/fixtures/identity_repos/ (new): scripts that build each case
  fresh per test run (not committed static fixtures — they're
  sensitive to git version).

### Unit tests (crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/identity.rs)

- test_identity_from_regular_repo
- test_identity_from_worktree
- test_identity_with_detached_head
- test_identity_with_orphan_branch
- test_identity_with_no_remote
- test_identity_with_corrupted_config (handles gracefully)
- test_identity_with_shallow_clone

### Logging

- target: mcp_agent_mail::core::identity
- event 'git_identity_detected' { project_path, repo_name,
  branch_name, remote_url_masked, duration_ms }
- remote_url_masked: strip user:password@ from the URL before logging.

### Integration test

- tests/integration/identity_migration.rs:
  Matrix of all 8 cases above; diff libgit2 result vs CLI result
  (the CLI reference invocations live in test-only code, wrapped in
  run_git_locked so they don't segfault).

### Fallback

- Identity is heuristic: if libgit2 fails, we return None (same as
  original CLI path's fail-silent). We do NOT attempt CLI fallback.
- Log WARN once per process if we see >5 None returns in a row on
  distinct paths — that's a signal that libgit2 is mis-configured
  (most likely vendored-libgit2 not linked).","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:07:49Z"},{"id":1478,"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"CLAIMED by RubyKnoll","created_at":"2026-04-20T01:57:46Z"},{"id":1481,"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"COMPLETED. identity::git_cmd rewritten to route through crate::git_cmd::GitCmd. Same signature, same stdout semantics, same None-on-nonzero-exit behavior. Removed unused std::process::Command import. This is a WRAP-form migration — the fuller libgit2 rewrite of all 10+ git_cmd call sites (rev-parse --show-toplevel, --git-common-dir, config --get, remote get-url, rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) is deferred because the per-site semantic mapping is non-trivial and the WRAP already eliminates the self-race surface.         .arg(\"-C\").arg(workdir)

that adds a race surface we don't need, because the libgit2 path
handles every case we care about.

  `try_restore_index_to_head` still recovers.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` must be clean.

## Non-goals

- Do not refactor the enclosing function. Just delete the shell-out.
  Minimal change, small diff, easy to revert.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:35:36.554904518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T01:56:00.688641809Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["easy-win","libgit2","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.5","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:35:36.554904518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.5","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:39:48.047901073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.5","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.3.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:49:51.723965357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1404,"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"## REVISION v2: Pre-deletion verification checklist + tests

 {"id":"br-8ujfs.3.6","title":"C6: Audit and migrate share/hosting.rs + share/detection.rs","description":"# C6: Audit and migrate share crate git calls

## Current sites

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/hosting.rs:236`:
  presumed `git rev-parse HEAD` or similar for bundle metadata.
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/detection.rs:532`: presumed
  `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` or remote detection.

## Why

Share operations are infrequent but can run during other traffic
(compose bundle while swarm is active). If migration is cheap, do it
for consistency. If the call is too niche to migrate cleanly, at
least wrap in `run_git_locked` (Track D).

## Method

1. Read both sites and classify: does the value come straight out of
   libgit2 (rev-parse, remote url, root path) or does it need
   something only the CLI gives us (porcelain output, custom
   merge-driver behavior)?
2. If libgit2 covers it: migrate.
3. If not: wrap in `run_git_locked`. Track D will catch it anyway but
   surfacing it here avoids missing a site.

  is equivalence-preserving only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:35:36.888317143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:49:54.217437804Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["libgit2","share"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.6","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:35:36.888317143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.6","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:39:48.275919409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.6","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.3.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:49:51.905810380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.6","depends_on_id":"br-8ujfs.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:49:54.216581701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1405,"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"## REVISION v2: Concrete site enumeration + tests

}

    fixture: &GitParityFixture,

    name: &str,
)

### Performance baselines

- Harness captures baseline timings on fresh CI runs and stores them
  in docs/GIT_PARITY_BENCHMARKS.md.
- Each migration bead (C2/C3/C4) references the baseline and asserts
  < 3x regression tolerance.
- If a bead improves performance (likely for C2/C3), update the
  baseline in the same commit.

### Gating on CLI availability

- Harness skips the CLI half of each assertion if:
    - `git` not on PATH

  explicit 'cli_skipped' annotation in output. CI treats this as

  actually has the number of commits claimed).

### Logging (target: test_harness::git_parity)

- INFO 'parity_case_start' { name, fixture_kind }

- ERROR 'parity_case_fail' { name, cli_result, libgit2_result, diff }
- DEBUG 'cli_skipped'      { name, reason }","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:08:44Z"},{"id":1469,"issue_id":"br-8ujfs.3.7","author":"ubuntu","text":"CLAIMED by RubyKnoll","created_at":"2026-04-20T01:48:11Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-8ujfs.3.8","title":"C8: Validate assumption — prove libgit2 is immune to the 2.51.0 index race","description":"# C8: Validate the libgit2-immunity assumption

## Why this bead exists

The ENTIRE Track C migration assumes libgit2 does NOT mmap .git/index
the same way CLI git does, and therefore libgit2 reads are race-immune
against concurrent CLI git writes. If this assumption is wrong, all
the Track C migrations buy us nothing — we're just swapping one racy
read path for another.

This bead PROVES or DISPROVES the assumption with a targeted test
BEFORE we start migrating. If it fails, we escalate and rethink.

## Hypothesis

libgit2 uses its own index representation loaded via read() + parse,
not mmap+pointer-walk. Therefore, a concurrent CLI git `index.write`
that truncates-and-rewrites the file causes libgit2 to see a
truncated file and return an error, NOT a segfault.

## Experiment

tests/integration/libgit2_index_race_immunity.rs:

1. Set up a tempdir repo with 100 committed files.
2. Parent process:
    - Spawns N=20 reader threads running libgit2 operations in a tight
      loop for 30 seconds:
        - `Repository::open`
        - `repo.statuses(None)`  (walks index)
        - `repo.index()?.iter().count()`  (walks index entries)
    - Spawns M=10 writer subprocesses running real CLI git in a tight
      loop:
        - `git add <file>; git reset <file>` (rewrites index)
    - Tallies per-reader: total ops, errors, panics.
3. At end of window:
    - Assert: no panics (libgit2 errors are fine; panics are not).
    - Assert: no SIGSEGV on ANY reader thread.
    - Assert: if errors > 0, they are all graceful ('invalid index
      file' or similar git2::Error variants).
    - Report total ops across readers and error rate.
4. Instrumentation:
    - Catches panics via std::panic::catch_unwind.
    - Captures signal handlers to detect if our OWN reader threads
      crashed.

## Acceptance

- If SIGSEGV observed: HYPOTHESIS REJECTED. Halt Track C migrations.
  File a P0 bead to rethink (Track C becomes 'wrap in retry' instead
  of 'migrate').
- If only graceful errors: HYPOTHESIS CONFIRMED. Track C proceeds.
- If zero errors at all: HYPOTHESIS STRONGLY CONFIRMED. libgit2 is
  fully race-immune via its read semantics.

## System under test

- Run with system git = 2.51.0 (required; the bug we care about).
- Run with system git = 2.50.2 as control.
- Both runs produce an artifact:
    tests/artifacts/libgit2_immunity/<git_version>/<ts>/report.json

## Dependencies

This is the FIRST bead to run in Track C. C1 (audit) can run in

Replace each `Command::new(\"git\").arg(\"-C\").arg(repo)...` pattern
with `GitCmd::new(repo).args(...).run()`.

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-guard/src/lib.rs` — see Track E's rationale.
  Guard stays unwrapped.

  tempdirs and don't hit user repos.
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/` — fixtures use real
  subprocess git on purpose. They run single-threaded in CI.

### Per-site allow-list (with rationale)

Written into the header comment of scripts/lint_git_shellouts.sh (D2):

    # SITES THAT MAY SHELL OUT DIRECTLY (no run_git_locked):
    # - crates/mcp-agent-mail-guard/src/lib.rs  (runs inside pre-commit
    #   hook; same pid as the git commit that invoked it; cannot race
    #   against itself; wrapping would deadlock)
    # - crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/**  (fixtures use raw
    #   subprocess git on purpose for python-reference parity)
    # - crates/*/benches/**                          (benchmark fixtures)
    # - crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/git_lock.rs   (the helper itself)

### Migration recipe (mechanical)

For each WRAP-classified site:

  BEFORE:
    let output = Command::new(\"git\")
        .arg(\"-C\").arg(repo)
        .args([\"rev-parse\", \"HEAD\"])
        .output()?;

  AFTER:
    let output = mcp_agent_mail_core::git_lock::GitCmd::new(repo)
        .args([\"rev-parse\", \"HEAD\"])
        .run()?;

- `-C` removed (GitCmd handles cwd).
- Binary resolution moves to AM_GIT_BINARY (A5).
- Locking + retry + metrics come automatically.

### Tests (per site)

- Before change: capture behavior in a targeted unit test.
- After change: same test must pass. If it doesn't, investigate
  (GitCmd should be behavior-preserving in every case).
- Integration test: tests/integration/wrap_parity.rs asserts every
  wrapped site produces identical output on a synthetic repo.

### Logging

- All wrapped calls now emit run_git_locked events (per B4). No
  additional logging needed at call sites unless the caller already
  had its own tracing — in which case keep it and let run_git_locked's
  events flow as well.

### Verification

- rg check: `rg -n 'Command::new\\(\"git\"\\)' crates/` returns only
  allow-listed hits. Runs in G1 quality gate and as the D2 lint.","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:09:02Z"}]}
